subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: This Verification Method is applicable to components with a resistance coefficient of variation of not more than 40%.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Where the calculated resistance coefficient of variation is less than 10%, then a minimum value of 10% must be used.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The resistance model for the component must be established by taking into account variability due to material properties, fabrication and construction processes, structural modelling and time dependent effects.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Annual action models must be determined for all reasonably expected actions and combinations of actions in accordance with Table B1V1a.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Compliance with B1P1(2) is verified for the design of a component where—

B1V1 of NCC 2022 may apply instead of B1V1 of NCC 2025 until 1 year after the adoption date of NCC 2025.
When determining appropriate combinations of actions to meet the requirements of B1V1(5)(b)(ii), consideration must be made for those given in AS/NZS 1170.
B1V1 may be used for all materials and actions to determine compliance with B1P1(2).
Requirements for values of 𝛽 can be found in Table B1P1a, Table B1P1b and Table B1P1c.
